mechanism_narrative: Sustained real GDP growth reflects expanding aggregate demand, which drives corporate earnings and supports higher equity prices over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If GDP growth turns negative for two consecutive quarters and S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0% signals a tightening labor market, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which lifts equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE falls below 5%, the S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances over a rolling 2-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.5% and CPI inflation is below 2% annually, real monetary conditions are highly accommodative, compressing discount rates and boosting equity valuations. The joint occurrence amplifies the positive effect.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 forward 20-day return is negative in more than 40% of instances when this condition is triggered over a 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and supply-side strength, which supports corporate earnings and equity prices.
failure_condition: If over any 3-year period, INDPRO month-over-month growth and S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positively correlated (Spearman rank coefficient < 0.1), reject.

mechanism_narrative: A strong monthly increase in retail sales signals robust consumer spending, raising immediate earnings expectations and boosting equity prices over the short term.
failure_condition: If in the 5 trading days after a trigger, the S&P 500 produces negative returns in more than 50% of occurrences, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly increases in both nonfarm payrolls (+200k) and housing starts (+50k) signal synchronized labor and housing market strength, amplifying confidence in sustained economic growth and driving equity prices higher.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month increase > 200k and HOUST month-over-month increase > 50k
failure_condition: If the average forward 20-day return following joint triggers is not positive and statistically significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Once the federal funds rate rises above 0.25% after a prolonged near-zero period, the onset of a monetary tightening cycle raises discount rates and reduces equity valuations over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If during periods when FEDFUNDS > 0.25% and rising, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in CPI annual inflation above 2% triggers expectations of imminent monetary tightening, which depresses equity prices over a 60-trading-day horizon as markets price in higher discount rates.
failure_condition: If after CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds 2%, the S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of cases over a 5-year rolling window, reject.
